name: Get It Done Law
description_short: Get It Done Law is a nationwide legal services network offering bankruptcy, DUI, traffic, criminal, family, personal injury, and employment law assistance through licensed attorneys.
description_long:
Get It Done Law operates as a legal services referral and coordination network with a stated mission to provide personalized, affordable legal assistance through a nationwide network of licensed attorneys. The company markets itself as a comprehensive legal solution provider rather than a single-practice firm, positioning intake coordinators and support staff to match clients with appropriate legal professionals across multiple practice areas.

Beyond bankruptcy, Get It Done Law advertises services spanning DUI and traffic defense, criminal law, immigration law, personal injury, divorce and family law, lemon law, employment law, workers' compensation, motor vehicle accidents, and social security disability. The website emphasizes rapid response, 24/7 availability through three phone lines, and free consultations across all advertised practice areas. Their stated strategy combines personalized attention with efficiency, using a nationwide attorney network rather than maintaining a single practice location.

The company differentiates itself through its multi-practice breadth, nationwide network model, and emphasis on speed ("Get It Done Right, Get It Done Fast"). Marketing language centers on client-centric service, experienced attorneys, and tailored solutions. The company highlights three core values: "Experienced – Committed – Client-Centric" and notes a mission prioritizing affordability alongside personalized service.

While the website presents comprehensive legal services and a nationwide network, potential clients should note that this appears to be a legal referral/coordination service rather than a single law firm. The breadth of practice areas advertised (13+ distinct legal specialties) is unusually wide for a single practice, suggesting reliance on partner attorneys. Bankruptcy shoppers should verify that referred attorneys are licensed in their jurisdiction and maintain appropriate malpractice insurance. The company's financial structure, attorney vetting process, and conflict-of-interest policies are not disclosed on the website.
pros: 24/7 availability with three dedicated phone lines including toll-free number (888) 908-8107
Free consultations offered across all practice areas
Nationwide network of licensed attorneys for clients in different states
Specialization in bankruptcy law with dedicated bankruptcy services section
Multi-practice legal services allowing clients to potentially address related legal needs (e.g., bankruptcy plus employment or family law issues)
Established intake and paralegal support staff for case coordination
Advertises rapid resolution and swift case handling as core service model
cons: Website lacks transparency about attorney licensing, credentials, or disciplinary history for referred attorneys
No disclosure of fee structure, cost estimates, or whether attorneys charge flat fees or hourly rates for bankruptcy services
Unclear whether this is a law firm or referral service; the distinction affects accountability and attorney responsibility
No client testimonials, case results, or success rates provided on the website
Unusually broad practice areas (13+ specialties) raise questions about depth of expertise versus volume-based referral model
best_for: Consumers seeking bankruptcy counsel in states where Get It Done Law has partner attorneys
Clients with multiple legal issues (bankruptcy plus personal injury, family law, or employment matters) who prefer single-point coordination
Those prioritizing availability and quick initial consultation over established single-practice law firm reputation
diagnosis: Get It Done Law is best for bankruptcy filers who need quick access to legal consultation and potentially have secondary legal needs (employment, family law, personal injury) that could be coordinated through a single referral network. The main caveat is verifying that referred attorneys are properly licensed in your jurisdiction, understanding their individual credentials and fee structures (not disclosed on this website), and confirming this referral model meets your need for direct attorney accountability in what is typically a complex, multi-month bankruptcy process.
services: Bankruptcy law services and financial fresh start planning
Personal injury claims and compensation recovery
Social Security Disability (SSDI/SSI) benefits and appeals
